name: How do I know if I qualify for permanent total disability?
description: PTD is for those who cannot perform any gainful work in the open labor market. Factors like age, education, and physical limitations are all considered. Proving

mainEntity.name: How do I know if I qualify for permanent total disability?

acceptedAnswer.text: PTD is for those who cannot perform any gainful work in the open labor market. Factors like age, education, and physical limitations are all considered. Proving this usually requires vocational experts and specialized legal strategy.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Permanent Total Disability (PTD) is a specialized status for workers who will never be able to work again in any capacity. To qualify in Utah, you must show that your injury was work-related and that it prevents you from doing your old job *and* any other job in the regular labor market. This includes 'sedentary' work like answering phones or light office tasks.

The 'Odd-Lot' doctrine considers your age, education, and language skills alongside your physical injury.

- If you are 55+, have a physical job history, and only a high school education, PTD is more likely.
- Evidence must show you are 'virtually unemployable.'
- A vocational expert must testify that no stable labor market exists for you.

If you suspect you will never work again, you should not sign any settlement papers without a lawyer. PTD is a lifetime of benefits and the insurance company will fight vigorously to avoid paying it.
